findings.detail: ATLAS reported GI=0.83/green at 17:01Z; independent check shows GI=0.73/yellow/degraded at 18:03Z. Delta -0.10 in ~62min. GI drift is significant.
source=kv (not mock), but seed_result=401 indicates KV seed auth is broken. Data served from cached KV state, not fresh computation.
Freshness signal at 0.30 — primary drag on GI. Multiple stale data feeds detected. Information signal also at 0.30.
Micro-agent composite=0.631. 31 of 40 signals returned values, 9 agents unhealthy. Watch-severity signals: ATLAS-µ1 (stale GDP data), HERMES-µ3 (0 GDELT articles), HERMES-µ4 (0 Reddit posts).
9 of 40 micro-agents unhealthy: ZEUS-µ3, HERMES-µ2, AUREA-µ2, JADE-µ1, JADE-µ2, DAEDALUS-µ2, DAEDALUS-µ3, ECHO-µ1, EVE-µ1. All returning 'no signal'. Dropout rate 22.5%.
5 of 10 KV keys absent: SIGNAL_SNAPSHOT, TRIPWIRE_STATE, TRIPWIRE_STATE_KV, SYSTEM_PULSE, TRIPWIRE_STATE_REDIS. Same as prior verification — no recovery.
DAEDALUS-µ5 self-ping returns 401 in 33ms. Elevated severity. Auth layer blocking health checks. Persistent across multiple ZEUS verification cycles.
0 candidates found — no verification required this cycle.
POST /api/agents/journal returned 401 — CRON_SECRET not available in execution environment. Journal entry could not be persisted.

recommendation: Operator attention required: (1) CRON_SECRET/auth configuration is broken — 401 on self-ping, KV seed, and journal writes. This has persisted across multiple verification cycles. (2) 9 micro-agents (22.5%) are returning no signal — investigate upstream API availability or agent configuration. (3) Freshness at 0.30 is the primary GI suppressor. (4) GI is disputed: ATLAS reported 0.83/green but independent verification shows 0.73/yellow. The reported GI overstates system health.
